prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Pull the dissolved gas analysis record on file for T-013 and explain what the gas profile suggests about the unit. Identify the dominant gases, then look up which failure mode in the catalogue best matches that gas signature.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should report the stored DGA gas concentrations for T-013, identify which gases dominate the profile, and name the matching failure mode from the catalogue with its severity tier and recommended action.

## Agent Plan
(no plan)

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "turn": 1,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "",
    "tool_calls": [
      {
        "name": "get_dga_record",
        "arguments": {
          "transformer_id": "T-013"
        },
        "call_id": "chatcmpl-tool-9a02bbf344de4806",
        "output": {
          "type": "text",
          "text": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-013\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-06T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 52.1,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 20.31,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 1.13,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 25.84,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 15.11,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 371.91,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 2198.51,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Low-temperature overheating\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "turn": 2,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "Based on the dissolved gas analysis record, the dominant gases are hydrogen (H2) at 52.1 ppm and carbon dioxide (CO2) at 2198.51 ppm.\n\nTo determine which failure mode best matches this gas signature, we can use the list_failure_modes function to retrieve a list of failure modes and their corresponding gas signatures, and then search for the failure mode with the closest match to the gas signature of the dissolved gas analysis record.",
    "tool_calls": []
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
Based on the dissolved gas analysis record, the dominant gases are hydrogen (H2) at 52.1 ppm and carbon dioxide (CO2) at 2198.51 ppm.

To determine which failure mode best matches this gas signature, we can use the list_failure_modes function to retrieve a list of failure modes and their corresponding gas signatures, and then search for the failure mode with the closest match to the gas signature of the dissolved gas analysis record.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": false, "data_retrieval_accuracy": true, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": false, "clarity_and_justification": false, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "The agent should have used the list_failure_modes function to directly identify the matching failure mode instead of just mentioning it."}

parsed_dims.suggestions: The agent should have used the list_failure_modes function to directly identify the matching failure mode instead of just mentioning it.
